整套首页
unit_10 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_10

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 483 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Ultimately, the young idealists (i) __________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii) __________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii) __________.Ultimately, the young idealists (i) __________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii) __________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten
salutarytributesubvert
salutary: standard unit headword | tribute: standard unit headword | subvert: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
obfuscateadducenaturedesign
obfuscate: standard unit headword | adduce: standard unit headword | nature: standard unit headword | design: standard unit headword
#3 SE
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the _______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the _______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
subjectsubject matter
subject: standard unit headword | subject matter: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
Needing an advocate who would be both precise and succinct, they rejected McLintock, whose inveterate ______ would automatically preclude meeting those requirements.Needing an advocate who would be both precise and succinct, they rejected McLintock, whose inveterate ______ would automatically preclude meeting those requirements.
答案：E　prolixity
succinctmeet
succinct: standard unit headword | meet: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not___—__ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist please, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not___—__ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist please, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.
答案：B　an expressive
fluidaffirm
fluid: standard unit headword | affirm: standard unit headword
#6 SE
While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such _______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such _______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive
genericaffirmative
generic: standard unit headword | affirmative: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)______ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)______ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)______ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)______ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)______ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)______ illness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to
placeboinert
placebo: standard unit headword | inert: standard unit headword
#8 SE
Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend
feministdownplay
feminist: standard unit headword | downplay: standard unit headword
#9 SE
It is commonly said that each person’s aesthetic taste is__—_—, yet that notion overlooks a large body of shared aesthetic preferences. [@M(SARS: 5k2/MGRE)It is commonly said that each person’s aesthetic taste is__—_—, yet that notion overlooks a large body of shared aesthetic preferences. [@M(SARS: 5k2/MGRE)
答案：C, E　distinct; idiosyncratic
idiosyncraticacquired
idiosyncratic: standard unit headword | acquired: standard unit headword
#10 TC TRIPLE
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive
tediouskeen
tedious: standard unit headword | keen: standard unit headword
#11 SE
Progressive and reactionary populist movements are not necessarily ______: each may, and usually does, possess the features of the other.Progressive and reactionary populist movements are not necessarily ______: each may, and usually does, possess the features of the other.
答案：A, D　dichotomous; contradictory
subversivepopulist
subversive: standard unit headword | populist: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable

Thus, the path to the truth is decidedly a (iii) __________ one.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　intuitively obvious; inevitable; sinuous

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial

Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii)______ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　slowed; drag on

答案：B　mendacity

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic

答案：E　disingenuous
lament

Blank 2

答案：E　plastic

#25 TC TRIPLE
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
frenzy
frenzy: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　baseless; untenable

Blank 3

The controversy about Alexander the Great’s personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i)______: all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii)______ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander’s alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great’s personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i)______: all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii)______ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander’s alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand
perish

答案：A　synonym for

But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

#51 TC TRIPLE
Fifteen years ago, the author of this scholarly monograph would have (i) founda university press willing to publish it. But today specialized books of this sort are (ii) species. Their main purchasers—university libraries—have far less money to spend on these items than they once did. Computerized catalogues, subscription content, expensive scientific journals, exploding storage costs: all these demands are (iii) library budgets.Fifteen years ago, the author of this scholarly monograph would have (i) founda university press willing to publish it. But today specialized books of this sort are (ii) species. Their main purchasers—university libraries—have far less money to spend on these items than they once did. Computerized catalogues, subscription content, expensive scientific journals, exploding storage costs: all these demands are (iii) library budgets.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　readily; an imperiled; putting tremendous
explode
explode: standard unit headword

答案：C　natter

Another argument for the (ii)___—saof analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (ii) language. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao. The author of this biography gives an accurate and (i)______ account of the subject’s life story, but all of this carefully assembled detail fails to compensate for the general lack of (ii)______ in her writing.The author of this biography gives an accurate and (i)______ account of the subject’s life story, but all of this carefully assembled detail fails to compensate for the general lack of (ii)______ in her writing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　selective; vivacity
subject
subject: standard unit headword
#102 SE

答案：A, F　unfounded; baseless

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the establish view.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

affirm: standard unit headword

答案：B　esoteric

Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i) a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii) the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii) illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

nature: standard unit headword

Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)______.

答案：B　generality

This (i) is (ii) , as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii) of surviving DNA.At a recent conference on ancient DNA, one presentation opened with the claim that the field was now mature and could move ahead with confidence. This (i) is (ii) , as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　optimism; unfounded; corrupted nature

#160 TC DOUBLE
The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative

答案：A　incontrovertible

But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles

K. Saho notes that it is difficult to fully (i)______ many of the oral accounts on which the study relies. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i) __________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii) __________: after all, gold is (iii) __________ in its bulk form. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." Blank 2
Blank 3

#207 SE
Anne Carson’s book Nox is, very deliberately, ______ literary object––the opposite of an e-reader, which is designed to vanish in your palm as you read on a train.Anne Carson’s book Nox is, very deliberately, ______ literary object––the opposite of an e-reader, which is designed to vanish in your palm as you read on a train.
答案：B, D　a cumbersome; an unwieldy

#208 TC SINGLE
Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.
答案：C　self-defeating

答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

答案：C　active

That idea is (ii) __________: the vast majority of nature's secrets have yet to be revealed.People tend to think of science as a gradual, steady accumulation of facts that has been ongoing for centuries. 答案：C　recede

Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. For example, mountain glaciers tend to be (iii)______: once established, they increase the reflectivity of the surface, thus lowering temperatures and allowing more ice to form.There are two opposing theories about mountain formation and climate over the past 40 million years: either the surge of mountain building (i)______ the global cooling, or vice versa. The first of these two theories asserts that widespread mountain building cooled the earth as a result of the (ii)______ mountains and climate. 答案：C, E, I　caused; feedback between; self-perpetuating

With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. Blank 3

Blank 2

Blank 2
答案：C, E　palliative; curative

#353 SE
An architect of Koolhaas’ (i) might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact, what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) _-_—- For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other. [#MAARS: sk#Z/MGRE)An architect of Koolhaas’ (i) might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact, what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) _-_—- For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other. [#MAARS: sk#Z/MGRE)
答案：A, F　unbridled ambition; solipsistic
design
design: standard unit headword
#354 TC TRIPLE
The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated
design
design: standard unit headword
#355 SE
We should (i) a history built on need and desire. The present always uses the past for its own purposes, but the best historical writing attempts to (ii) that inescapable impulse in order to understand history in its own terms.We should (i) a history built on need and desire. The present always uses the past for its own purposes, but the best historical writing attempts to (ii) that inescapable impulse in order to understand history in its own terms.
答案：B, E　be wary of; contain
impulse
impulse: standard unit headword
#356 TC TRIPLE
In recent years in India, the works of such figures as Mohandas Gandhi, Vallabhbhai Patel, Subhas Chandra Bose, and Rabindranath Tagore have all benefited from (i) __________ interpretations of each man's life and thought. Jawaharlal Nehru, by contrast, has been subjected to (ii) __________ that border on caricature, portrayed as (iii) __________ a one-dimensional view of science and a vacuous universalism.In recent years in India, the works of such figures as Mohandas Gandhi, Vallabhbhai Patel, Subhas Chandra Bose, and Rabindranath Tagore have all benefited from (i) __________ interpretations of each man's life and thought. Jawaharlal Nehru, by contrast, has been subjected to (ii) __________ that border on caricature, portrayed as (iii) __________ a one-dimensional view of science and a vacuous universalism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　more nuanced; simplifications; a mouthpiece for
subject
subject: standard unit headword
#357 SE
By dramatizing the lives of her subjects with candor, sympathy, and a healthy strain of skepticism, director Esther Bell offers an (i) the whimsy and solipsism (ii) to much of what passes for independent filmmaking.By dramatizing the lives of her subjects with candor, sympathy, and a healthy strain of skepticism, director Esther Bell offers an (i) the whimsy and solipsism (ii) to much of what passes for independent filmmaking.
答案：A, F　antidote to; endemic

subject: standard unit headword
#358 TC TRIPLE
There is something seductive about (i) __________ judgments in the arts. When a critic peremptorily announces that Titian's canvases are the greatest ever painted or that Mondrian and Malevich are the only truly radical artists of the twentieth century, we enjoy seeing all the confusions of taste swept aside, even though we may (ii) __________ reservations about the (iii) __________ nature of these opinions.There is something seductive about (i) __________ judgments in the arts. When a critic peremptorily announces that Titian's canvases are the greatest ever painted or that Mondrian and Malevich are the only truly radical artists of the twentieth century, we enjoy seeing all the confusions of taste swept aside, even though we may (ii) __________ reservations about the (iii) __________ nature of these opinions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　absolutist; harbor; categorical
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#359 SE
The idea that arguments based on probability are deployed only by (i) persons is (ii) even among scientists. Perhaps such an evaluation has partly to do with the origins of the subject in the mathematics of gambling games, not generally regarded as pastimes for people of goodThe idea that arguments based on probability are deployed only by (i) persons is (ii) even among scientists. Perhaps such an evaluation has partly to do with the origins of the subject in the mathematics of gambling games, not generally regarded as pastimes for people of good
答案：B, D　disreputable; widespread

subject: standard unit headword
#360 TC TRIPLE
Unlike news reports, theater isn't expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what's real and what's (iii) __________.Unlike news reports, theater isn't expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what's real and what's (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#361 SE
Meegan Garber argues that the typical suburban lawn, which was originally designed to (i) homes even as it enforced the distance between them, may be considered (ii) domestic space: the whole neighborhood is invested in the appearance of each neighbor's yard.Meegan Garber argues that the typical suburban lawn, which was originally designed to (i) homes even as it enforced the distance between them, may be considered (ii) domestic space: the whole neighborhood is invested in the appearance of each neighbor's yard.
答案：B, E　connect; common
design
design: standard unit headword
#362 TC TRIPLE
Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion
subject
subject: standard unit headword
#363 SE
The songwriter's (i) nature can lead her audiences to forget that her songs are about painful experiences just as often as they are about (ii) ones.The songwriter's (i) nature can lead her audiences to forget that her songs are about painful experiences just as often as they are about (ii) ones.
答案：B, E　jocose; exultant
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#364 TC TRIPLE
The artist's portraits of the social elite, with their luscious sensuality and insistent prettiness, have often been regarded with sly amusement, if not outright (i) __________. One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.The artist's portraits of the social elite, with their luscious sensuality and insistent prettiness, have often been regarded with sly amusement, if not outright (i) __________. One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter

outright: standard unit headword
#365 SE
Although weeds often (i) and are (ii) human activities, their cussedness and refusal to play by human rules makes them subversive and the very essence of wildness.Although weeds often (i) and are (ii) human activities, their cussedness and refusal to play by human rules makes them subversive and the very essence of wildness.
答案：A, F　follow; dependent on
subversive
subversive: standard unit headword
#366 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
subject
subject: standard unit headword
#367 SE
George Eliot's novels were calculated to inspire sympathy in the reader for characters from a broad range of social classes, but her design was not (i) : Eliot hoped her fiction would have a (ii) effect, spurting readers to show charity for those oppressed by the economic inequalities of her age. [#(EARS: 5kHZIHGRE)George Eliot's novels were calculated to inspire sympathy in the reader for characters from a broad range of social classes, but her design was not (i) : Eliot hoped her fiction would have a (ii) effect, spurting readers to show charity for those oppressed by the economic inequalities of her age. [#(EARS: 5kHZIHGRE)
答案：C, F　exclusively affective; galvanizing

#368 TC TRIPLE
Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　boom; impose a specious coherence on; complexity
subject
subject: standard unit headword
#369 SE
As an essayist, Reed is an equal-opportunity combatant who pulls no punches. One strength in his approach is that this (i) can be disarmingly refreshing. On the downside, however, Reed sometimes surrenders too quickly to his more (ii) impulses.As an essayist, Reed is an equal-opportunity combatant who pulls no punches. One strength in his approach is that this (i) can be disarmingly refreshing. On the downside, however, Reed sometimes surrenders too quickly to his more (ii) impulses.
答案：C, D　aggressive frankness; polemical

But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.The notion of popular science or of the public understanding of science is very much a concern in today's world, where we can see (i) __________ between science and nonscience. But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　a clear demarcation; disparate; consensus on

subject: standard unit headword
#371 SE
The biographer laments the __________ that abound in texts from antiquity; they are so prevalent, she says, that they nearly amount to a "conspiracy of silence."The biographer laments the __________ that abound in texts from antiquity; they are so prevalent, she says, that they nearly amount to a "conspiracy of silence."
答案：D, E　elisions; lacunae

#372 TC TRIPLE
Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　uncomfortable with; nefarious; an invasion of

nature: standard unit headword
#373 SE
The approach initially adopted by historians of women, which revolved around the __________ of women in earlier periods, significantly enriched historical knowledge but inadvertently limited the feminist historian's task to simply recovering previously ignored facts.The approach initially adopted by historians of women, which revolved around the __________ of women in earlier periods, significantly enriched historical knowledge but inadvertently limited the feminist historian's task to simply recovering previously ignored facts.
答案：A, E　visibility; recognition
feminist
feminist: standard unit headword
#374 TC TRIPLE
In 1859 the editor of one newspaper in the western United States lamented that "the popular idea of Western Literature" consisted of such cliched topics as "log cabins, rough speech, dare-devil boldness, bear-hunting and cornhusking." With Easterners still (i) __________ the representation of the West, western writers had to publish in the East in order to succeed, despite a (ii) __________ literary scene in many western cities. Not surprisingly, this editor urged Westerners to (iii) __________ representations of the West created by Easterners.In 1859 the editor of one newspaper in the western United States lamented that "the popular idea of Western Literature" consisted of such cliched topics as "log cabins, rough speech, dare-devil boldness, bear-hunting and cornhusking." With Easterners still (i) __________ the representation of the West, western writers had to publish in the East in order to succeed, despite a (ii) __________ literary scene in many western cities. Not surprisingly, this editor urged Westerners to (iii) __________ representations of the West created by Easterners.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　controlling; burgeoning; reject
lament
lament: standard unit headword
#375 SE
Concerned to upend the standard top-down approach to game design, Flanagan calls on game designers to __________ the typical model with one that is less hierarchical.Concerned to upend the standard top-down approach to game design, Flanagan calls on game designers to __________ the typical model with one that is less hierarchical.
答案：B, F　supplant; replace

design: standard unit headword
#376 TC TRIPLE
The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative
idiosyncratic
idiosyncratic: standard unit headword
#377 SE
Fairy shrimp, having evolved to live in one of nature's most __________ environments, are able to capitalize on the highly temporary pools of water found in spring woodlands.Fairy shrimp, having evolved to live in one of nature's most __________ environments, are able to capitalize on the highly temporary pools of water found in spring woodlands.
答案：A, F　transient; ephemeral

#378 TC TRIPLE

As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification

Rather than fully embracing a position of __________, Alurista rejects the view that his poetry provides only negative critique; rather, he aims to reconstruct a Chicano consciousness uncolored by views imposed through colonization.Rather than fully embracing a position of __________, Alurista rejects the view that his poetry provides only negative critique; rather, he aims to reconstruct a Chicano consciousness uncolored by views imposed through colonization.
答案：B, E　oppositionality; protest

Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　dispelled; friendly; costs

Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.
答案：B, D　pliant; malleable

#382 TC TRIPLE
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

explode: standard unit headword
#383 SE
In the early 1960s, Hofstadter observed that intellectuals tend to view business as the "classic enemy" of intellectualism and that businessmen themselves have so long accepted this role that by now their __________ is seen as a fact of nature.In the early 1960s, Hofstadter observed that intellectuals tend to view business as the "classic enemy" of intellectualism and that businessmen themselves have so long accepted this role that by now their __________ is seen as a fact of nature.
答案：A, D　enmity; hostility

Thus today in the (iii)______ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)______ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)______. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence

The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Blank 3

She, too, believes in those things.Flaherty understands that biological theories of artistic creation may (i) __________ people who cherish the idea that art comes from something other than the action of neurotransmitters, and she spends much time trying to shepherd us through the difficulty of accepting that the mind is actually the brain, a physical organ. Blank 3

#443 SE
Physicians today increasingly rely on endoscopic surgery, replacing large scalpels and clamps with cameras and with tools whose ______ enables them to snake into the body through tiny holes.Physicians today increasingly rely on endoscopic surgery, replacing large scalpels and clamps with cameras and with tools whose ______ enables them to snake into the body through tiny holes.
答案：A, D　flexibility; suppleness

#444 TC TRIPLE
In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original

idiosyncratic: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　aspersions; slurs

#446 TC TRIPLE
When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　ambiguity; misleading; veracity

#448 TC TRIPLE
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

#450 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
